name: react-router-expert
description: Architectural opinions and recommendations for React Router 6+ in enterprise PWA applications

route_configuration_style.title: Route Configuration Style: Objects vs JSX
route_configuration_style.description: Choose between route objects and JSX syntax for defining routes
route_configuration_style.rationale: Route objects provide better TypeScript support, easier testing, and centralized configuration. JSX routes are more familiar but less testable.

context.when_applicable: All enterprise React applications with React Router 6+
context.when_not_applicable: Simple prototypes or apps migrating from React Router 5 with minimal refactoring time

options.name: Route Objects (Recommended)
options.description: Define routes as plain JavaScript objects
pros: Better TypeScript support and type inference
Can be generated dynamically from API/config
Easier to compose and merge route configs
cons: Less familiar to developers coming from React Router 5
options.use_when: Building enterprise applications requiring testability, type safety, and maintainability

options.name: JSX Routes
options.use_when: Migrating from React Router 5 or building simple applications

guard_implementation.title: Route Guard Implementation Strategy
guard_implementation.description: How to implement authentication and authorization checks for routes
guard_implementation.rationale: Route-level guards provide clear security boundaries and prevent unauthorized route access at the routing layer, rather than within components.

context.when_applicable: All enterprise applications with authentication and role-based access

options.name: Guard Components with Outlet (Recommended)
options.description: Create guard components that check auth and render Outlet or Navigate
pros: Guards are routes themselves (part of route hierarchy)
DRY - guards are reusable across route branches
Can show loading states during auth checks
cons: Guards add extra components to React tree
options.use_when: Building enterprise PWAs with layered security (auth + roles + permissions)

options.name: Loader Functions with Redirects
options.description: Use route loader functions to check auth and throw redirect responses
pros: Data loading and auth checking in one place
cons: Harder to compose multiple security checks
Can't show loading UI during auth check
options.use_when: Simple auth checks with no layered authorization

options.name: Component-Level Checks (Not Recommended)
options.description: Check auth within each protected component
cons: Not DRY (repeated checks in every component)
Security holes if checks are forgotten
Component loads before redirect (flash of content)

decision_factors: Complexity of authorization (simple auth vs roles vs permissions)
Need for loading states during auth checks

url_state_management.title: State Management: URL vs Redux vs Memory
url_state_management.description: Where to store different types of application state in relation to routing
url_state_management.rationale: URL state enables deep linking and sharing, Redux state persists across navigation, memory state is lost on navigation. Each has appropriate use cases.

context.when_not_applicable: Never - always need to decide where state lives

options.name: URL Query Params (Recommended for UI State)
options.description: Store filters, sorting, pagination, search in URL query params
pros: Shareable - users can copy/paste URLs to colleagues
Bookmarkable - users can save filtered views
Deep linkable - direct access to specific filtered state
cons: URL can become long with many params
Sensitive data shouldn't be in URL
Must validate params (user can manipulate URL)
options.use_when: Filters, sorting, pagination, search terms, selected tabs, expanded/collapsed state

options.name: Redux/Global State
options.description: Store state in Redux that persists across navigation
pros: Persists when navigating away and back
Can be used across multiple routes
options.use_when: User preferences, draft work, shopping cart, notification state, authentication

options.name: Component Memory (useState)
options.description: Store state in component with useState/useReducer
options.use_when: Form input, modal open/close, accordion expand/collapse, hover state

decision_factors: Does state need to be shareable?
Does state need to persist across navigation?
Does state need to persist across page refresh?
Is state sensitive (shouldn't be in URL)?

code_splitting_strategy.title: Route-Based Code Splitting Strategy
code_splitting_strategy.description: Which routes to code-split and which to load eagerly
code_splitting_strategy.rationale: Code splitting reduces initial bundle size but adds network latency. Balance is needed based on route usage patterns.

options.name: Aggressive Code Splitting (Recommended for Enterprise)
options.description: Split all routes except home/login, preload on hover
pros: Users only download what they use
cons: Slight delay on first visit to split route (mitigated by preloading)

options.name: Conservative Code Splitting
pros: No loading delay for common routes
options.use_when: Fast internal networks, desktop-first, small applications
